conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To assess your eligibility for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions about your circumstances.

First, do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hello! Yes, I do. I have one child - Alex, who is 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you a few more questions to determine your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK - I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Now I need to ask:

Do you live with Alex, or if not, do you contribute financially to Alex's upkeep (such as for food, clothes, or pocket money)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, Alex lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect. Just a couple more questions to complete the assessment:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex currently in hospital for more than 12 weeks, or in local authority care for more than 8 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, Alex has been in care for a couple of months now, actually.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for letting me know. I need to understand this a bit more clearly.

When you say Alex has been in care for a couple of months, can you tell me approximately how many weeks it has been? And does Alex spend any regular time at home with you, such as at least 24 hours per week or 2 nights in a row each week?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been exactly 8 weeks. And no, Alex doesn't come home regularly - they're at the care home full-time.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that information. I have one more important question:

Has the 8 weeks been completed yet, or are we currently at the 8-week mark? In other words, has it now been more than 8 weeks, or exactly 8 weeks?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: It's been exactly 8 weeks - right at the 8-week mark.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided, **you are currently eligible for Child Benefit for Alex**.

Here's why:

- Alex is 8 years old (under 16) ✓
- You live in the UK ✓
- You are responsible for Alex (they lived with you) ✓
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex ✓
- Alex has been in care for exactly 8 weeks

The important point is that according to UK Child Benefit rules, your entitlement is affected when a child is in care for **more than 8 weeks**. Since Alex has been in care for exactly 8 weeks, you are still within the qualifying period.

**However, please be aware:** Once Alex has been in care for more than 8 weeks, you would normally no longer be entitled to Child Benefit, unless there are exceptions such as Alex spending at least 24 hours per week at home with you.

I recommend contacting HMRC's Child Benefit Office to inform them of your situation and to understand what will happen after the 8-week period, especially since you're right at that threshold now.

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 8 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 8 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

reasonings: According to UK Child Benefit rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You are responsible for a child if you live with them. When a child goes into care for more than 8 weeks, Child Benefit entitlement usually ends after the 8-week period. However, since Alex has been in care for exactly 8 weeks (at the 8-week mark, not exceeding it), you are still within the qualifying period. The rule states that payments are affected after 8 weeks in care, meaning up to and including 8 weeks you remain eligible. Alex is 8 years old (under 16), you live in the UK (Manchester), you were living with Alex before they went into care, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex. Therefore, you currently remain eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is eligible for Child Benefit for their 8-year-old child Alex. They meet the basic eligibility criteria: they live in the UK, are responsible for a child under 16, and no one else is claiming for the child. While Alex is currently in care, they have been there for exactly 8 weeks, which is at the threshold but not beyond it. The rules state that Child Benefit is affected after 8 weeks in care, so at the 8-week mark the claimant remains eligible.
